message:
feat(approval): Cx-first consume_for_plan + attach_to_decision — approval.rs trail complete (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Final slice on the approval.rs Cx-first trail. Adds the last 3 of
7 ApprovalStore entry points.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `ApprovalStore::consume_for_plan_with_cx(cx, allow_once_code,
    input, plan_hash)` — pure delegate to
    `consume_for_plan_with_context_with_cx` with `None` audit
    context, mirroring the legacy delegation.

  * `ApprovalStore::consume_for_plan_with_context_with_cx(cx,
    allow_once_code, input, plan_hash, audit_context)` — Cx-first
    parallel of `consume_for_plan_with_context`. Same checkpoint
    structure as tick-61's `consume_with_context_with_cx` but
    with an additional plan_hash match gate before the audit
    checkpoint. TOCTOU contract preserved: mismatched plan_hash
    still CONSUMES the token (used_at set) but returns `None` and
    no audit is recorded.

  * `ApprovalStore::attach_to_decision_with_cx(cx, decision, input,
    summary)` — Cx-first parallel of `attach_to_decision`. Pure
    delegate: when `decision.requires_approval()`, routes through
    `issue_with_cx` so the underlying token issue honors caller
    cancellation; otherwise returns the decision unchanged
    without touching storage (no checkpoint needed on that branch
    because it's a pure decision transformation).

Regression test (new) `approval_rs_cx_first_trail_complete`:

  * Exercises all 3 new entry points in a single test to cap the
    trail with an end-to-end integration check.

    Path 1: `consume_for_plan_with_cx` shorthand succeeds on
      matching plan_hash.
    Path 2: `consume_for_plan_with_context_with_cx` with explicit
      `ApprovalAuditContext { correlation_id: ..., .. }` fires
      the audit branch.
    Path 3: `attach_to_decision_with_cx` on a RequireApproval
      decision routes through `issue_with_cx`, matches the
      RequireApproval variant pattern to extract the attached
      code, and verifies the resulting token is consumable via
      `consume_with_cx` (proves the end-to-end
      attach→consume round-trip works under Cx-first).
    Path 4: `attach_to_decision_with_cx` on an Allow decision is
      a pure pass-through — the returned decision is the same
      Allow variant with no approval attached.

approval.rs Cx-first trail COMPLETE. All 7 ApprovalStore public
async methods have Cx-first siblings:
  * `issue`                          ✓ (tick 59)
  * `issue_for_plan`                 ✓ (tick 60)
  * `consume`                        ✓ (tick 61)
  * `consume_with_context`           ✓ (tick 61)
  * `consume_for_plan`               ✓ (tick 62, this commit)
  * `consume_for_plan_with_context`  ✓ (tick 62, this commit)
  * `attach_to_decision`             ✓ (tick 62, this commit)

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * approval:: — 98 passed / 0 failed (tick 61: 97 -> tick 62: 98,
    +1 new end-to-end trail-complete test; all 94 legacy approval
    tests still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (1m32s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
